calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica Dell"Osso for Carol Johnson.

Ms. Dell Osso stated that Ms. Harrington does not intend to appear today, that Ms. Harrington felt that since she filed a memorandum of points and authority that it does not require an appearance, and that Ms. Harrington has a hearing in another county. Ms. Dell'Osso also stated that there is no bill for the escape taxes, that they estimate the taxes to be about $1,200.00, that the Children have different Mothers and that Adrienne and Aleta are not eligible for the Grandparent Grandchild exclusion. In addition, Ms. Dell'Osso asked that Ms. Harrington's memorandum of points and authorities be disregarded, stated that it was not filed in timely manner as ordered by Judge Sing, that no response time was available and that the taxes will only assess 1/6th of the property. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-01-2013 Continued To Jul-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica De'Osso for Carol Johnson. Alicia Gamez specially appeared on behalf of Amy Harrington for Adrienne Ireton, Aleta Bryant and Charles Sean Norris (Beneficiaries).

Ms. Del'Osso argued that the Administrator had full authority under the Independent Administraion of Estates Act (IAEA) and Probate Code 10514 to obtain a loan with out prior Court authority. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the supplemental assessment property taxes being paid on a pro rata basis by those Beneficiaries that do not qualify for the parent-child exclusion. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Bradley.

The matter is now continued to July 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Harrington to file a Memorandum of Points and Authorities with respect to the property taxes. Said Memorandum of Points and Authorities is to be filed by July 10, 2013. A reply is to be filed July 17, 2013. The Probate Examiner is to notify Counsel.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Continued To Sep-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica Dell'Osso for Carol Johnson. Clara Coats present in pro per.

The Court inquired why the filing of the petition was delayed. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that she usually she files a declaration to address such issues, that the parties have been talking about a resolution, and that she was requesting a 30 day continuance to try and reach a resolution regarding an appointment of the permanent representative, etc. In addition, Ms. Dell'Osso addressed the delay in filing the petition for probate of will stating that the decedent died in May of 2011, that the first few weeks after her Mother's death her client was dealing with the loss, that her client was not focusing on what needed to happen next in terms of the administration of the estate, and that her client remembered that her Mother had shown her a holographic instrument which her client located in her Mother's home. Ms. Dell'Osso also stated at the time her client did not have Counsel and was unsure if the document was a valid testate, that her office was retained shortly thereafter, that once her client sought their advice the document was presented to the Court, and that under those circumstances they don't believe that the delay was unreasonable. Ms. Coats responded stating that she didn't have access to the house and that she had wanted to see if she could locate a will since the will didn't show up right away. Based on the explanation provided by Counsel and no contradiction to what Counsel said by Ms. Coats the Court found that the delay was not unreasonable and that the petition for probate can go forward. Ms. Coats stated that she saw the will when she came down to the Court and that she did not recognize her Mother's handwriting. The Court stated that Ms. Dell'Osso would need to file Ms. Del submit a proof of holographic instrument by somebody than Ms. Johnson. Ms. Coats stated that she filed the petition for special admninsitration because she wanted access to the house say her good-byes. The Court stated that pursuant to the Probate Code that reason for petition for letters of special administration doesn't suffice and that it was taking that petition off calendar. Ms. Coats stated that she filed a will contest yesterday and provided a copy for the Court to review. Ms. Dell'Osso reviewed the copy of the will contest in open court. The Court continued the petition for letters of administration and the petition for probate of will filed by Carol Johnson to September 14, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
